project.objective: Who were the parents of Reuben Spencer Spriggs, born 6 November 1898 in Maddock, Benson County, North Dakota?

questions.question: Who were the parents of Reuben Spencer Spriggs, born 6 November 1898 in Maddock, Benson County, North Dakota?
questions.rationale: This is the primary research question directly derived from the project objective. Reuben Spencer Spriggs (L64C-QQX) has no parent links in tree.gedcomx.json. Given his 1898 birth in Benson County, ND, the 1900 US census (showing him as a ~1-year-old in his parents' household), North Dakota birth registrations, and the 1910 census (showing him at age ~11 in SD, possibly still with parents) are the highest-yield starting records. His 1917–1918 WWI draft card and 1998 obituary are already in the tree and may corroborate parentage information.

exhaustive_declaration.justification: All planned record sets have been searched or justifiably skipped. Eight log entries (log_001 through log_008) represent the full search effort: 1900 ND census (nil in Benson County; partial statewide — Reuben's family had relocated to Iowa); 1910 SD census (found Reuben in household of John W. Spriggs and Charlotte Spriggs — direct parentage evidence); WWI draft card (Reuben self-names 'Father — Wm. Spriggs' — direct parentage evidence, name-form conflict resolved); obituary (negative — no parents named, expected as predeceased); 1915 SD census (father's birthplace Iowa, mother's birthplace Norway — independent indirect corroboration); Find A Grave (negative — no parents listed); Washington County marriage record (John W. Sprigg + Charlotta Westby married 19 Aug 1898, Spokane, WA — confirms couple as unit, identifies Charlotte's maiden name as Westby). Four independent sources directly or indirectly address parentage from different informants and record types (census enumerator, self-report, state census, county marriage official). The California death certificate (pli_006) was skipped due to no Ancestry subscription; the Benson County church records (pli_009, browse-only unindexed) were skipped because existing evidence is sufficient for a defensible conclusion. No additional searches are likely to materially change the conclusion.
exhaustive_declaration.stop_criteria: Evidence from four independent sources (1910 census, 1915 SD census, 1918 WWI draft card, 1898 Washington marriage record) converges on the same parents without contradiction. Conflict between 'John W.' and 'Wm.' name forms resolved. All extractable assertions are linked to persons. No additional record sets with high yield probability remain unexamined within available repositories.

items.rationale: The 1900 US census is the highest-yield record for identifying Reuben's parents. He was born November 1898 in Benson County, ND; the June 1900 enumeration should find him as a ~1.5-year-old in his parents' household, providing both parents' names, ages, and birthplaces. The Benson County 1900 census is 92% indexed on FamilySearch.

The tree places Reuben in Belle Prairie Township, Beadle County, SD in 1910 (age ~11). At that age he was likely still in a parental household. Searching the 1910 census for Reuben and the broader Spriggs family in South Dakota may directly name his parents and reveal when and why the family relocated from North Dakota.

Reuben's WWI draft registration card (ARK K68M-VHS) is already in the tree but not yet extracted. WWI draft cards required the registrant to name their nearest relative (often a parent or spouse). Extracting this record will reveal any parentage information Reuben himself reported.

Reuben's 1998 obituary (ARK QKLM-51PS) is already in the tree but not yet extracted. Obituaries commonly name surviving siblings, children, and sometimes parents. Extracting this record may identify parents' names or surviving siblings who share the same parents.

The ND 1915 state census is indexed on FamilySearch (619,486 records). If Reuben (age ~16) or his parents were still in North Dakota in 1915, this census will show the family household and confirm parentage. Also useful for finding any Spriggs family members still in Benson County.

California death certificates name the decedent's parents on lines 11-12 (father's name and birthplace; mother's maiden name and birthplace). Reuben died 21 May 1998 in Riverside, CA. This is a high-value secondary source for parentage — the informant (likely a child) may have known the grandparents' names. Search California Death Index and certificate images on Ancestry.

North Dakota County Marriages 1872-2000 (17,428 records indexed on FamilySearch) should include the parents' marriage record, which provides the mother's maiden name — often absent from census and death records. Once the parents are identified by name from the 1900 census or other sources, search for their marriage to confirm the couple as a unit and supply the mother's pre-marital surname.

The Find A Grave index entry for Reuben (ARK Q231-HPSQ) at Maplewood Cemetery, Iroquois, Kingsbury County, SD is in the tree but not yet extracted. Cemetery entries and Find A Grave memorials often list family relationships, spouses, and parents. Reuben's wife Nora is also buried at Maplewood; searching for other Spriggs burials at the same cemetery may identify parents or siblings.

Two Norwegian-language church record collections exist for Benson County (Esmond 1830-1958, Flora 1820-1997), both digitized but only ~34-36% indexed. If the Spriggs family belonged to a Norwegian or Scandinavian congregation in Benson County, baptismal, marriage, and membership records may name parents. Search FamilySearch catalog and browse image groups 100881428 and 100881427 as a FAN-cluster supplement if census evidence proves insufficient.

log.notes: Found 3 Spriggs adults in Benson County 1900: John H Spriggs (b. Dec 1868, DC, 'single'), Jesse L Spriggs (b. Apr 1876, WV, 'single'), Silas B Spriggs (b. Sep 1877, OH, 'single'). None appear to be the head of household with an infant born Nov 1898. Live reads confirmed none had children. Reuben Spencer Spriggs not found in Benson County 1900 census; family likely relocated to Iowa by June 1900 (confirmed by sibling Lillian born 1900 in Iowa).

Broadened to all ND; found 'Ruben Spriggs' household in Grand Forks (b. Dec 1865, MN; wife Florence L. b. May 1872, PA; children born 1895, 1896, Sep 1898 in ND). This is NOT Reuben's father — youngest child Leroy J. born Sep 1898 and Reuben Spencer born Nov 1898 cannot share same mother; different family. Reuben Spencer Spriggs still absent from 1900 ND census. Family had moved to Iowa by June 1900 (Lillian born 1900 in Iowa in 1910 household).

Top result (score 4.39, 5-star tree match to L64C-QQX): 'Rueben S Spriggs' in 1910 census, Prairie Belle Twp, Beadle County, SD, in 'Household of John W Spriggs.' Live record read of ark:/61903/1:1:MPXD-MZC confirmed full household: father John W. Spriggs (b. 1872, Iowa), mother Charlotte Spriggs (b. 1877, Norway), siblings Lillian F (b. 1900, Iowa) and Leonard W (b. 1907, SD). Record contains explicit ParentChild relationships linking both parents to Rueben S. PARENTS IDENTIFIED: Father = John W. Spriggs (b. ca.1872, Iowa); Mother = Charlotte Spriggs (b. ca.1877, Norway). Sibling born in Iowa ca.1900 explains absence from ND 1900 census.

query.note: Initial ND-scoped search returned 0 results; broadened to William + Charlotte + US nationwide 1888-1902

log.notes: Initial ND-scoped search for 'John Spriggs' marriage returned 0 results. Broadened to nationwide search for 'William Spriggs' + spouse 'Charlotte', 1888-1902. Top result: 'John W Sprigg' (b.1872) + 'Charlotta Westby' (b.1876), married 19 August 1898, Spokane City, Spokane, Washington (ark:/61903/1:1:QPMT-VPJ5, collection 1534448). Birth year 1872 for groom matches I1 exactly; 'Charlotta Westby' matches Charlotte Spriggs (b.~1877 Norway, I2) — Westby is a Norwegian surname. Marriage date 19 Aug 1898 is 79 days before Reuben's birth 6 Nov 1898; consistent with pre-marital conception. Record also lists Charlotta's parents: father 'O Weslby' and mother surname 'Hansen'. MAIDEN NAME IDENTIFIED: Charlotte's maiden name is Westby (Charlotta Westby)."

conflicts.description: Name-form discrepancy for Reuben Spencer Spriggs's father: the 1910 U.S. census (src_001) records the head of household as 'John W Spriggs' (a_001), while Reuben's 1918 WWI draft registration card (src_004) has him naming his nearest relative and employer as 'Father — Wm. Spriggs' (a_052, a_053). The first name 'John' (census) versus 'Wm.' (abbreviation for William, draft card) appears contradictory.
conflicts.disputed_attribute: Father's name: 'John W. Spriggs' (1910 census, a_001) vs. 'Wm. Spriggs' (1918 WWI draft card, a_052/a_053)
conflicts.identity_question: Is 'John W. Spriggs' (1910 census head of household, Reuben's father) the same individual as 'Wm. Spriggs' named as Reuben's father on his 1918 WWI draft card?
conflicts.independence_analysis: The two conflicting sources are independent: src_001 (1910 census) was created by a census enumerator who visited the Spriggs household in Prairie Belle Twp, Beadle Co., SD; src_004 (1918 WWI draft card) was created by Reuben Spencer Spriggs himself as registrant with local board registrar M. E. Harmon. The 1910 enumerator and the 1918 registrant (Reuben) are different informants with no derivation relationship. A third independent source — the 1915 SD state census (src_005/a_063) — records the father's Iowa birthplace without naming him, providing a neutral corroborative bridge between both name forms.
conflicts.resolution_rationale: Both records refer to the same individual, John William Spriggs. The reconciliation relies on the common 19th–early 20th century American naming practice of using one's middle name as the preferred everyday name. 'John W.' on the 1910 census reflects the legal first name as recorded by the enumerator; 'Wm.' on the 1918 draft card reflects the middle name 'William' by which Reuben's father was known in daily life. Both 'W.' and 'Wm.' abbreviate 'William,' making the connection explicit. Supporting evidence: (1) identical surname Spriggs; (2) consistent SD location (Beadle Co. 1910, Kingsbury Co. 1918 — adjacent counties); (3) same family role (father of Reuben Spencer Spriggs); (4) independent corroboration of Iowa birthplace in both src_001 (a_002) and src_005 (a_063). No other candidate Spriggs father appears in any record. Preferred assertion is a_001 (census/legal name form).
conflicts.weighing_analysis: src_001 (1910 census) is weighted more heavily for legal name: enumerators were trained to record legal given names, and the full household context is internally consistent and independently corroborated. src_004 (WWI draft card, a_052/a_053) reflects Reuben's personal and affective knowledge of his father's preferred name; everyday name usage vs. legal name is the standard explanation for this pattern. The 1915 SD census (src_005/a_063), by independently confirming Iowa as the father's birthplace without using either name, serves as a neutral anchor that confirms the same man is referenced in both conflicting records.

proof_summaries.narrative_markdown:
## Who Were the Parents of Reuben Spencer Spriggs (1898–1998)?

**Conclusion:** The preponderance of available evidence strongly indicates that Reuben Spencer Spriggs, born 6 November 1898 in Maddock, Benson County, North Dakota, was the son of **John William "William" Spriggs** (born about 1872 in Iowa) and **Charlotte (Westby) Spriggs** (born about 1876–1877 in Norway).

### Research Scope

Eight searches were conducted across four record types and multiple repositories: the 1900 and 1910 U.S. federal censuses, the 1915 South Dakota state census, Reuben's 1918 World War I draft registration card, his 1998 obituary, Find A Grave, and Washington County marriage records (1855–2008). Two searches yielded negative results (Benson County, ND 1900 census — family not located; obituary — parents not named). The California death certificate (Ancestry subscription required) and unindexed church records for Benson County were not searched; neither omission materially affects the conclusion given the convergent evidence from four independent sources.

### Evidence

**1910 U.S. Federal Census** (original; "United States, Census, 1910," FamilySearch, ark:/61903/1:1:MPXD-MZC): The 1910 census for Prairie Belle Township, Beadle County, South Dakota, records "John W. Spriggs" as head of household and "Charlotte Spriggs" as his wife, with "Rueben S. Spriggs," age ~11, born in North Dakota, listed as their child (a_001, a_006, a_017, a_018). This is the primary direct evidence that John W. and Charlotte Spriggs are Reuben's parents. John W. Spriggs is recorded as born about 1872 in Iowa (a_002); Charlotte is born about 1877 in Norway (a_007). The source is an original government schedule; the informant is an unidentified household member (indeterminate information quality per pre-1940 census convention).

**1918 World War I Draft Registration Card** (original; "United States, World War I Draft Registration Cards, 1917–1918," FamilySearch, ark:/61903/1:1:K68M-VHS): Reuben Spencer Spriggs personally completed his draft card on 12 September 1918 in Huron, Beadle County, SD. He named his nearest relative as "Father — Wm. Spriggs" at Esmond, Kingsbury County, SD (a_053), and entered "for self & help Father Wm. Spriggs" in the employer field (a_052). This is primary direct self-reported evidence of the father's identity from Reuben himself. Together, these two fields constitute one evidence unit (same informant: Reuben); they are not two independent corroborations of each other, but they are independent of the 1910 census (different informant, different record type, different year).

**Name-form discrepancy resolved (conflict c_001):** The father appears as "John W. Spriggs" in the 1910 census and "Wm. Spriggs" (William) in the draft card. These sources are independent (different informants, different jurisdictions, different years). The reconciliation is the well-documented American naming practice of using one's middle name in daily life: "John W." is the enumerator-recorded legal name; "Wm." reflects the middle name William by which the father was known to his son. Evidence anchoring both name forms to the same man: identical surname, South Dakota location across both records (adjacent Beadle and Kingsbury counties), identical family role (Reuben's father), and Iowa as the father's birthplace recorded independently in the 1910 census (a_002) and the 1915 SD state census (a_063). No other Spriggs candidate appears in any examined record.

**1915 South Dakota State Census** (original; "South Dakota, State Census, 1915," FamilySearch, ark:/61903/1:1:MMH5-9TH): "Spencer Spriggs," age 16, is enumerated in Belle Prairie Township, Beadle County, SD. His card records his father's birthplace as Iowa and his mother's birthplace as Norway (a_063, a_064). This independently corroborates the father's Iowa origin and the mother's Norwegian origin as found in the 1910 census, from a different record created by a different enumeration process five years later. Information quality is secondary (a respondent reporting parental birthplaces from family knowledge).

**1898 Washington County Marriage Record** (original; "Washington, County Marriages, 1855–2008," FamilySearch, ark:/61903/1:1:QPMT-VPJ5): A Spokane County marriage register entry dated 19 August 1898 documents the marriage of "John W. Sprigg" (born 1872) and "Charlotta Westby" (born 1876) at Spokane City, Washington (a_085–a_090). The marriage date is 79 days before Reuben's birth on 6 November 1898, establishing the couple as married before his birth and consistent with paternity within the marriage. Rail service between Spokane and the Dakotas existed in 1898, making the subsequent relocation to Benson County, ND for the birth plausible logistically. This record provides the mother's maiden name (Westby — a Norwegian surname consistent with I2's Norway birthplace) and confirms the couple as a unit. The groom's birth year (1872) matches the father identified in the 1910 census exactly; the bride's birth year (1876) is within one year of the mother's census estimate (~1877). This record does not name Reuben and is therefore indirect evidence for the parentage question, though it independently confirms the couple's identity and relationship at a date consistent with his birth.

**Negative evidence:** The 1900 census search for Reuben's family in Benson County, ND, yielded no match; a broadened statewide search found a different, unrelated Spriggs family in Grand Forks County. Reuben's 1998 obituary names surviving family members but not his (long-predeceased) parents. Find A Grave lists no parents for Reuben's memorial. These negative results are consistent with the family's relocation from North Dakota between 1898 and 1910.

### Assessment

The evidentiary weight for father and mother differs and should be stated separately.

**Father (John William Spriggs):** Two independently sourced assertions directly identify John W. / William Spriggs as Reuben's father: (1) the 1910 census (a_017), with an indeterminate-quality informant (unidentified household member); and (2) Reuben's own 1918 draft card (a_052/a_053), constituting primary-quality self-report. These two sources are independent of each other (different informants, different record types, eight years apart). In addition, the 1915 SD state census (a_063) independently records the father's Iowa birthplace, corroborating the 1910 census's Iowa attribution from a separate enumeration. The father's identification is well-supported across two independently-sourced direct assertions.

**Mother (Charlotte Westby Spriggs):** Only one direct assertion independently identifies Charlotte as Reuben's mother: a_018 (1910 census, indeterminate quality). The maternal identification is corroborated indirectly by the 1915 SD state census (a_064 — mother's birthplace Norway, independent from 1910) and the 1898 Washington marriage record (a_087–a_090 — confirming Charlotte Westby as John W.'s wife 79 days before Reuben's birth). These indirect sources are strong corroboration but do not independently name Charlotte as Reuben's mother. The maternal link is therefore well-supported but carries more evidentiary weight through indirect corroboration than through multiple independently-sourced direct assertions.

No contradicting parentage evidence appears in any examined record. The only conflict (father's name form: "John W." vs. "Wm.") is resolved by middle-name usage evidence (c_001).

The conclusion is **probable** rather than proved because: (1) the California death certificate was not searched and may carry secondary parentage information from a child-informant who knew both parents' names; (2) the family was not located in the 1900 census, leaving no original-era household record placing Reuben with his parents before 1910; (3) no North Dakota birth record naming Reuben's parents was found; and (4) the maternal identification rests on one direct assertion of indeterminate quality with strong but indirect corroboration. These gaps do not undermine the convergent evidence but prevent a proved tier under the Genealogical Proof Standard.

**Based on the preponderance of available evidence, it is highly probable that Reuben Spencer Spriggs (born 6 November 1898, Maddock, Benson County, North Dakota) was the son of John William "William" Spriggs and Charlotte (Westby) Spriggs.**
